title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 May 2024.

items.title: **PlayStation London Studio** has officially closed its doors
bullets: Founded in 2002 and based in Soho, London, the first-party studio worked on The Getaway series and was responsible for the Singstar franchise.
It also developed numerous games for PlayStation’s EyeToy camera, Wonderbook, and PlayStation VR titles VR Worlds and Blood & Truth.

items.title: **IGN **Entertainment acquires Eurogamer, GI, VG247, Rock Paper Shotgun and more
bullets: New owner makes redundancies across the Gamer Network portfolio
Gamer Network’s publications are GamesIndustry.biz, Eurogamer (including six local language editions), Rock Paper Shotgun, VG247 and Dicebreaker.
The business also holds shares in Outside Xbox, Digital Foundry and Hookshot (which operates Nintendolife, PushSquare, PureXbox and Time Extension).

items.title: **Amazon Games** is opening its first European studio, helmed by a Ubisoft veteran
bullets: The studio will be led by Cristian Pana, former managing director at Ubisoft Bucharest.
Pana has over 20 years of experience and previously worked on Far Cry, The Division, and Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora.

items.title: **Little Kitty Big City** reaches 1m players on Game Pass
bullets: Double Dagger Studio's debut sold 100,000 units in two days following its May 9 launch
